---
title: "Party or Climate? The Polarization of the US Public’s Energy Preferences"
bibliography: /Users/matthewnowlin/Library/CloudStorage/Dropbox/refs.bib
csl: /Users/matthewnowlin/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UTArlington/01-RESEARCH/Manuscript-Files/csl/american-political-science-association.csl
fig-cap-location: top
fontfamily: ebgaramond
link-bibliography: false
format: 
  pdf:
    include-in-header: 
      text: |
        \usepackage{setspace}
        \usepackage{dcolumn}
        \usepackage{float}
        \usepackage{amsmath}
        \addtokomafont{disposition}{\rmfamily}
  docx:
    reference-doc: /Users/matthewnowlin/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UTArlington/01-RESEARCH/Manuscript-Files/reference.docx
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE, message=FALSE}
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = normalizePath(".."))
```

```{r load data and recodes}
#| echo: false
#| include: false
#| warning: false

# packages
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(broom)
library(patchwork)


# data
cueEnergyData <- read.csv("data/cueEnergyData.csv")

```


# Results 

```{r ols models}
#| message: false
#| include: false

# cue models 
summary(cuemodel1 <- lm(renewables.opinion ~ conRep + libDem, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (trump.cue == 1)))
summary(cuemodel2 <- lm(renewables.opinion ~ conRep + libDem, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (climate.cue == 1)))

coefs <- bind_rows(
  tidy(cuemodel1, conf.int = TRUE) %>% mutate(model = "Trump Cue"),
  tidy(cuemodel2, conf.int = TRUE) %>% mutate(model = "Climate Cue")
) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
  mutate(term = recode(term,
                       "conRep" = "Conservative\nRepublicans",
                       "libDem" = "Liberal\nDemocrats"))

p1 <- ggplot(filter(coefs, model == "Trump Cue"), aes(x = estimate, y = term, color = term)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_point(size = 4) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high), height = 0.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Conservative\nRepublicans" = "red",
                                "Liberal\nDemocrats" = "blue")) +
  labs(x = "", y = NULL,
       title = "How do you feel about the Trump administration’s goal of increasing the use of\nfossil fuels and decreasing the use of renewable sources in generating electricity?") +
  theme_minimal() + theme(legend.position = "none", plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11))


p2 <- ggplot(filter(coefs, model == "Climate Cue"), aes(x = estimate, y = term, color = term)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_point(size = 4) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high), height = 0.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Conservative\nRepublicans" = "red",
                                "Liberal\nDemocrats" = "blue")) +
  labs(x = "Coefficient Estimates", y = NULL,
       title = "How do you feel about increasing the use of renewable sources\nin generating electricity as a way to address climate change?",
       caption = "A 1 to 5 scale was used on both questions") +
  theme_minimal() + theme(legend.position = "none", plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11))

p1 / p2

ggplot(coefs, aes(x = estimate, y = term, color = term)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_point(size = 4) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high), height = 0.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Conservative\nRepublicans" = "red",
                                "Liberal\nDemocrats" = "blue")) +
  facet_wrap(~ model, ncol=1) +
  labs(x = "Coefficient estimate", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# models: data subset by condition  
summary(ffmodelTrump <- lm(alloc.fossil ~ conRep + libDem+trump.approval+male+white+edu+inc, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (trump.cue == 1)))
summary(ffmodelClimate <- lm(alloc.fossil ~ conRep + libDem+trump.approval+male+white+edu+inc, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (climate.cue == 1)))
summary(ffmodelControl <- lm(alloc.fossil ~ conRep + libDem+trump.approval+male+white+edu+inc, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (control == 1)))

summary(WindmodelTrump <- lm(alloc.wind ~ conRep + libDem+trump.approval+male+white+edu+inc, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (trump.cue == 1)))
summary(WindmodelClimate <- lm(alloc.wind ~ conRep + libDem+trump.approval+male+white+edu+inc, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (climate.cue == 1)))
summary(WindmodelControl <- lm(alloc.wind ~ conRep + libDem+trump.approval+male+white+edu+inc, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (control == 1)))

summary(SolarmodelTrump <- lm(alloc.solar ~ conRep + libDem+trump.approval+male+white+edu+inc, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (trump.cue == 1)))
summary(SolarmodelClimate <- lm(alloc.solar ~ conRep + libDem+trump.approval+male+white+edu+inc, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (climate.cue == 1)))
summary(SolarmodelControl <- lm(alloc.solar ~ conRep + libDem+trump.approval+male+white+edu+inc, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (control == 1)))

summary(HydromodelTrump <- lm(alloc.hydro ~ conRep + libDem+trump.approval+male+white+edu+inc, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (trump.cue == 1)))
summary(HydromodelClimate <- lm(alloc.hydro ~ conRep + libDem+trump.approval+male+white+edu+inc, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (climate.cue == 1)))
summary(HydromodelControl <- lm(alloc.hydro ~ conRep + libDem+trump.approval+male+white+edu+inc, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (control == 1)))

summary(NuclearmodelTrump <- lm(alloc.nuclear ~ conRep + libDem+trump.approval+male+white+edu+inc, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (trump.cue == 1)))
summary(NuclearmodelClimate <- lm(alloc.nuclear ~ conRep + libDem+trump.approval+male+white+edu+inc, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (climate.cue == 1)))
summary(NuclearmodelControl <- lm(alloc.nuclear ~ conRep + libDem+trump.approval+male+white+edu+inc, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (control == 1)))

# predictions
## +trump.approval+male+white+edu+inc
models <- list(
 ffmodelTrump <- lm(alloc.fossil ~ conRep + libDem, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (trump.cue == 1)),
 WindmodelTrump <- lm(alloc.wind ~ conRep + libDem, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (trump.cue == 1)),
 SolarmodelTrump <- lm(alloc.solar ~ conRep + libDem, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (trump.cue == 1)),
 HydromodelTrump <- lm(alloc.hydro ~ conRep + libDem, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (trump.cue == 1)),
 NuclearmodelTrump <- lm(alloc.nuclear ~ conRep + libDem, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (trump.cue == 1)),
 ffmodelClimate <- lm(alloc.fossil ~ conRep + libDem, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (climate.cue == 1)),
 WindmodelClimate <- lm(alloc.wind ~ conRep + libDem, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (climate.cue == 1)),
 SolarmodelClimate <- lm(alloc.solar ~ conRep + libDem, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (climate.cue == 1)),
 HydromodelClimate <- lm(alloc.hydro ~ conRep + libDem, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (climate.cue == 1)),
 NuclearmodelClimate <- lm(alloc.nuclear ~ conRep + libDem, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (climate.cue == 1)),
 ffmodelControl <- lm(alloc.fossil ~ conRep + libDem, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (control == 1)),
 WindmodelControl <- lm(alloc.wind ~ conRep + libDem, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (control == 1)),
 SolarmodelControl <- lm(alloc.solar ~ conRep + libDem, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (control == 1)),
 HydromodelControl <- lm(alloc.hydro ~ conRep + libDem, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (control == 1)),
 NuclearmodelControl <- lm(alloc.nuclear ~ conRep + libDem, data = cueEnergyData, subset = (control == 1))
 )

new_data <- data.frame(
  conRep = c(0,1),
  libDem = c(1,0))
#  trump.approval=mean(cueEnergyData$trump.approval),
#  male=mean(cueEnergyData$male),
#  white=mean(cueEnergyData$white),
#  edu=mean(cueEnergyData$edu),
#  inc=mean(cueEnergyData$inc)
#  )

results <- map_dfr(models, ~ as.data.frame(predict(.x, newdata = new_data, interval = "confidence")), .id = "model")

graphData <- expand_grid(
  treatment = c("Trump","Climate","Control"),
  source    = c("Fossil Fuels", "Wind", "Solar", "Hydro", "Nuclear"),
  party     = c("Liberal Democrats", "Conservative Republicans")
)

pred_df <- bind_cols(graphData, as_tibble(results))

pred_df$fit[1]
pred_df$lwr[1]

ggplot(pred_df, aes(x = treatment, y = fit, fill = party)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = lwr, ymax = upr),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
    width = 0.2
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ source, nrow = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Liberal Democrats" = "#2166AC",
                               "Conservative Republicans" = "#B2182B")) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Predicted share of energy mix (%)",
    fill = NULL,
    title = "Predicted Energy Mix by Political Beliefs and Cues",
    caption = "Bars show predicted values from OLS; whiskers are 95% confidence intervals."
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8, hjust = 0.5),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold")
  )


ggplot(pred_df, aes(x = treatment, y = fit, color = party, group = party)) +
  geom_line(position = position_dodge(width = 0.3), linewidth = 0.6, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_pointrange(
    aes(ymin = lwr, ymax = upr),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.3),
    size = 0.6
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ source, nrow = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Liberal Democrats" = "#2166AC",
                                "Conservative Republicans" = "#B2182B")) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Predicted share of energy mix (%)",
    color = NULL,
    title = "Predicted Energy Mix by Political Beliefs and Cues",
    caption = "Points are predicted values from OLS; vertical bars are 95% confidence intervals."
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8, hjust = 0.5),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold")
  )


gap_df <- pred_df |>
  select(party, treatment, source, fit) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = party, values_from = fit) |>
  mutate(gap = `Liberal Democrats` - `Conservative Republicans`) |>
  mutate(
    treatment = factor(treatment, levels = c("Control", "Climate", "Trump")),
    source    = factor(source, levels = c("Fossil Fuels", "Nuclear", "Hydro", "Wind", "Solar")),
    direction = if_else(gap > 0, "Dem", "Rep")
  )

ggplot(gap_df, aes(x = treatment, y = gap)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_segment(aes(xend = treatment, y = 0, yend = gap),
               color = "grey60", linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_point(aes(color = direction), size = 3) +
  facet_wrap(~ source, nrow = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Dem" = "#2166AC", "Rep" = "#B2182B"),
    labels = c("Dem" = "Democrats prefer more",
               "Rep" = "Republicans prefer more"),
    name = NULL
  ) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Predicted partisan gap (percentage points)\nLiberal Democrats − Conservative Republicans",
    title = "Partisan differences in preferred energy mix, by treatment",
    caption = "Points are predicted differences from OLS models fit separately by energy source."
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )


gap_sorted <- gap_df |>
  mutate(
    # Create a combined label for the y-axis
    label = paste0(source, " — ", treatment),
    # Sort by absolute gap, smallest to largest (so largest appears at top of plot)
    label = fct_reorder(label, abs(gap)),
    direction = if_else(gap > 0, "Dem", "Rep")
  )

ggplot(gap_sorted, aes(x = gap, y = label)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = 0, xend = gap, yend = label),
               color = "grey60", linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_point(aes(color = direction), size = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Dem" = "#2166AC", "Rep" = "#B2182B"),
    labels = c("Dem" = "Democrats prefer more",
               "Rep" = "Republicans prefer more"),
    name = NULL
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Predicted partisan gap (percentage points)\nLiberal Democrats − Conservative Republicans",
    y = NULL,
    title = "Where are the largest partisan gaps in preferred energy mix?",
    caption = "Sorted by absolute size of gap. Predicted values from OLS models fit by energy source."
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank()
  )

```


\newpage

# Online Methods 
\setcounter{table}{0}
\renewcommand{\thetable}{A\arabic{table}}

\newpage

# References
\setlength{\parindent}{-0.2in}
\setlength{\leftskip}{0.2in}
\setlength{\parskip}{8pt}
\vspace*{-0.2in}
\singlespace
\noindent
